message:
Apply rustfmt formatting to storage benchmark suite

Formatting standardization across the benchmark file:
- Reorder imports alphabetically (IdConfig before IdGenerator) to match
  rustfmt's canonical ordering for use statements.
- Consolidate multi-line function call arguments to single lines where
  they fit within line length limits, improving readability for simple
  generator.generate() calls that were unnecessarily spread across 7-8
  lines.
- Reformat closure bodies for better readability, particularly the
  collision check closure which now uses a block body style that makes
  the HashSet::contains call clearer.
- Use point-free style for content_hash mapping (issues.iter().map(content_hash)
  instead of issues.iter().map(|i| content_hash(i))) to reduce noise.
- Break long method chains (storage.search_issues().unwrap()) across
  multiple lines for consistency with project style guidelines.
- Expand criterion_group! macro invocations to multi-line format for
  better readability when listing multiple benchmark functions.

No behavioral changes; all benchmarks produce identical results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
